remaining_defects.title: Per-record adjudications are not recorded
remaining_defects.evidence: The three round-2 artifacts provide aggregate classification counts, methods, and a small number of representative or counterevidence anchors, but they do not bind each of the 1,212 source match records to one exact adjudication category. Consequently, the reported 987/180/45/0 classification cannot be independently recomputed from the artifacts.
remaining_defects.required_action: Emit a deterministic adjudication ledger with exactly one row per generated-match identity, including pattern_id, path, line, full_line_sha256, adjudication category, canonical cluster where applicable, and reviewer/pass identity. Validate uniqueness, exact coverage of all 1,212 source records, and recompute the merged counts from that ledger.

remaining_defects.title: Assigned-record-set hash method is not consistently declared
remaining_defects.evidence: Adjudication B records an assigned_record_set_sha256 but does not specify its canonicalization algorithm; adjudications A and C do not record equivalent assigned-set hashes. The source file hash and family partition are independently valid, but the lane evidence is not uniformly hash-bound.
remaining_defects.required_action: Define one canonical assigned-record serialization and emit its SHA-256 for all three lanes, preferably deriving lane membership from the per-record adjudication ledger.

saturation.required_next_step: Repair and validate the per-record adjudication genealogy; no additional source reading is required unless that reconciliation exposes unclassified or conflicting records.
saturation.scope_if_repaired: Pinned public revision and the six reviewed generated pattern families only; runtime, contracts, inaccessible history, and future revisions remain open.

release_effect: Release remains blocked. The bounded report and aggregate round-2 conclusion may remain candidates, but the project must not claim deterministic static saturation until SAT2-DEF-001 is repaired and independently reconciled.
